title: As My Career Lay Dying

jokes.text: Banana liqueur. I needed a drink a couple of hours ago. This was the only thing I could find.
jokes.setup: Someone asks what Hank is drinking
jokes.punchline: He's so desperate he's drinking banana liqueur, the most ridiculous possible alcohol choice
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Hank's pathetic state - only someone truly desperate would drink banana liqueur.

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: Banana liqueur. I needed a drink a couple of hours ago. This was the only thing I could find.

jokes.text: My breath smells like a monkey's asshole.
jokes.setup: Hank has been drinking banana liqueur
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely specific description of the aftermath
jokes.explanation: Vividly disgusting and perfectly captures Hank's lack of filter and current pathetic state.

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: My breath smells like a monkey's asshole.

jokes.text: There's a fire extinguisher there. It has a simple elegance, and yet it has strength.
jokes.setup: Hank says he's never really looked around the office closely before
jokes.punchline: He waxes poetic about the aesthetic qualities of a fire extinguisher
jokes.explanation: Beautifully absurd character moment - drunk Hank finding profound meaning in mundane office equipment with pretentious art-speak.

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: There's a fire extinguisher there. It has a simple elegance, and yet it has strength.

jokes.text: I thought when Larry announced his retirement from the show, I would wake up, because I have that nightmare almost every night.
jokes.setup: Hank can't believe the show is ending
jokes.punchline: Reveals he regularly has nightmares about Larry retiring
jokes.explanation: Dark and revealing character detail that shows Hank's deep insecurity and dependence on Larry. Well-structured revelation.

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: I thought when Larry announced his retirement from the show, I would wake up, because I have that nightmare almost every night.

jokes.text: Hard.
jokes.setup: Hank asks someone to pinch him harder to wake him from the 'nightmare'
jokes.punchline: Single word delivery suggesting inappropriate enjoyment
jokes.explanation: Maximum economy but relies on cheap innuendo. The awkward beat afterward is the real joke.

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: Hard.

jokes.text: I think the ratings were even higher for the super bowl than they were for the 3rd rock episode. A very special 3rd rock.
jokes.setup: Comparing Super Bowl ratings to a sitcom episode
jokes.punchline: Deadpan observation that the Super Bowl beat a 'very special episode' of 3rd Rock
jokes.explanation: Mild industry insider joke about TV ratings and 'very special episodes' but not particularly sharp.

dialogue.speaker: Jim Gray
dialogue.line: I think the ratings were even higher for the super bowl than they were for the 3rd rock episode. A very special 3rd rock.

jokes.text: Right in between Richard Simmons and jenny mccarthy.
jokes.setup: Boasting about being on The Tonight Show
jokes.punchline: The terrible booking placement between two lowbrow guests
jokes.explanation: Good structure - builds up the booking then deflates it with the embarrassing guest placement.

dialogue.line: Right in between Richard Simmons and jenny mccarthy.

jokes.text: Everyone knows you're the reason we lost football.
jokes.setup: Tension between Bob and Hank during their argument
jokes.punchline: Bob blames Hank for losing NFL coverage, a devastating professional accusation
jokes.explanation: Sharp character-driven insult. In sports broadcasting, losing NFL rights is career-damaging, making this a precise attack.

dialogue.speaker: Bob Costas
dialogue.line: Everyone knows you're the reason we lost football.

jokes.text: If you want to sit there and stare like a moron and breathe though your mouth, you can go sit in jenny jones' audience.
jokes.setup: Artie is berating Hank for being unprofessional
jokes.punchline: Specific insult about sitting in a trashy talk show audience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie insult - combines his crude directness with industry-specific contempt. Strong character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: If you want to sit there and stare like a moron and breathe though your mouth, you can go sit in jenny jones' audience.

jokes.text: You better climb back on board and towel off, because we're gonna finish the show in a blaze of glory.
jokes.setup: Artie's ship metaphor about Hank jumping off the sinking show
jokes.punchline: 'Towel off' - bizarrely specific addition to the maritime metaphor
jokes.explanation: Artie's mixed metaphors create absurd imagery. 'Towel off' is wonderfully specific and unexpected.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You better climb back on board and towel off, because we're gonna finish the show in a blaze of glory.

jokes.text: Just like when you hit on Michael landon's wife. He was dead. That woman was sitting shivah.
jokes.setup: Artie accuses Brian of making moves on people at inappropriate times
jokes.punchline: Specific example of hitting on a grieving widow during Jewish mourning ritual
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and specific. The shivah detail makes it both more shocking and more believable for this character.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Just like when you hit on Michael landon's wife. He was dead. That woman was sitting shivah.

jokes.text: You put your tongue in her mouth. I lost my balance. I was falling over, and I stuck my tongue out to break my fall.
jokes.setup: Artie accuses Brian of kissing the widow
jokes.punchline: Brian's completely absurd physical explanation for tongue-kissing
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant absurdist logic. The specific physics of 'sticking tongue out to break my fall' is comedic gold. Highly quotable and original.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: You put your tongue in her mouth.

dialogue.speaker: Brian
dialogue.line: I lost my balance. I was falling over, and I stuck my tongue out to break my fall.

jokes.text: Pure comedy Gold. Melt it, mint it, and ship it to fort knox.
jokes.setup: Artie says the show was great
jokes.punchline: Elaborate metaphor extending 'comedy gold' into actual gold processing
jokes.explanation: Classic Artie - takes a cliché and over-elaborates it. Good character voice but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Pure comedy Gold. Melt it, mint it, and ship it to fort knox.

jokes.text: Pussy-whipped. Flying right back to St. Louis.
jokes.setup: Asked about why Costas can't join them for lunch
jokes.punchline: Crude dismissal of Costas's family obligations
jokes.explanation: Pure Artie character moment - crude but economical. The St. Louis detail adds specificity.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Pussy-whipped. Flying right back to St. Louis.

jokes.text: Very talented... basketball player.
jokes.setup: Fred calling Magic Johnson 'very talented' like the others
jokes.punchline: The pause and qualification that he means basketball, not entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan correction. The pause suggests Fred almost got carried away with false praise.

dialogue.speaker: Fred de Cordova
dialogue.line: Very talented... basketball player.

jokes.text: Your boyfriend was over? Ignoring that.
jokes.setup: Hank mentions going home for 'a stiff one'
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate gay joke followed by Hank's quick dismissal
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry cheap shot and Hank's practiced dismissal shows their dynamic. Predictable but well-executed.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: Your boyfriend was over? Ignoring that.

jokes.text: I saw my face reflected in the, uh, in the cooktop, and I said... 'lemons.'
jokes.setup: Hank describes his moment of inspiration at home
jokes.punchline: Seeing his reflection in kitchen appliance leads to profound realization about lemons
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Hank moment - the mundane appliance reflection leading to life wisdom is beautifully ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: I saw my face reflected in the, uh, in the cooktop, and I said... 'lemons.'

jokes.text: lemons. When life gives you lemons, you make things with lemons.
jokes.setup: Hank had an epiphany looking at his reflection
jokes.punchline: His garbled version of 'when life gives you lemons, make lemonade'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank character comedy. He mangles the common saying in a way that reveals his mental processes.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: lemons. When life gives you lemons, you make things with lemons.

jokes.text: Little... little playlets, really.
jokes.setup: Hank describing his cruise ship acting experience
jokes.punchline: Diminutive, pretentious term for what were obviously amateur skits
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - Hank's pretentious language trying to elevate clearly amateur work.

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: Little... little playlets, really.

jokes.text: She stepped on every one of my fucking lines. Oh, I know. I saw that production. And you said you enjoyed it, right? Yeah. That's what I said.
jokes.setup: Hank complains about Ruth Buzzi ruining his performance
jokes.punchline: Larry admits he was lying when he said he enjoyed it
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline structure revealing both Larry's dishonesty and Hank's delusion about his talent.

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: She stepped on every one of my fucking lines.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: Oh, I know. I saw that production. And you said you enjoyed it, right?

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: That's what I said.

jokes.text: Move along, chinaman.
jokes.setup: Hank proudly recounts his one line from Kung Fu
jokes.punchline: The line is horribly racist and inappropriate by today's standards
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about casual racism in old TV. Hank's pride in delivering such a line reveals his character perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: Move along, chinaman.

jokes.text: I hate to be the one to rain on your parade, but doing a play with Ruth buzzi and saying, 'move along, chinaman,' do not an acting career make.
jokes.setup: Hank is excited about his acting prospects
jokes.punchline: Larry brutally deflates him by listing his pathetic credentials
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure and typical Larry deflation of Hank's dreams. The grammar flip adds wit.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: I hate to be the one to rain on your parade, but doing a play with Ruth buzzi and saying, 'move along, chinaman,' do not an acting career make.

jokes.text: Well, there's no shame in doing porno.
jokes.setup: Hank says he got an acting gig
jokes.punchline: Larry assumes it must be pornography
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry putdown - assumes the worst about Hank's prospects. Good misdirection but somewhat predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: Well, there's no shame in doing porno.

jokes.text: Hey, now. We barely know each other.
jokes.setup: Hank as a plumber being asked to 'snake her drain'
jokes.punchline: He adds his catchphrase to what's meant to be a sexual innuendo in the script
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - Hank can't help but insert his catchphrase even in scripted acting, missing the innuendo.

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: Hey, now. We barely know each other.

jokes.text: He cannot act. I know. Don't worry. Don't worry.
jokes.setup: Hank is struggling with the audition
jokes.punchline: Director admits to producer that Hank can't act, but they're not worried
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe moment about Hank's lack of talent, but the casual dismissal adds to the humiliation.

dialogue.line: He cannot act.
I know.
Don't worry.
Don't worry.

jokes.text: I think they're gonna want to hear the 'hey, now' when they see me.
jokes.setup: Director asks Hank not to say his catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Hank insists the audience expects it from him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank delusion - he thinks people know him for his catchphrase and expects it even in other roles.

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: I think they're gonna want to hear the 'hey, now' when they see me.

jokes.text: And now I have to sit next to that... that... that... that woman.
jokes.setup: Hank ranting about his failed audition
jokes.punchline: His inability to even say Lea Thompson's name, just 'that woman' with disgust
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hank's wounded ego and inability to process criticism professionally.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: And now I have to sit next to that... that... that... that woman.

jokes.text: Jesus, Sidney, will you please get up, and will you help me here?
jokes.setup: Hank is on the phone with his agent
jokes.punchline: His agent apparently fell down during the call, adding to Hank's career disasters
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Hank's bad luck - even his agent is literally falling down on the job.

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: Jesus, Sidney, will you please get up, and will you help me here?

jokes.text: So... what, did you hire a black man?
jokes.setup: Pat Sajak says they 'went a different way' in casting
jokes.punchline: Hank's inappropriate assumption about what 'different way' means
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable character moment showing Hank's casual racism and cluelessness about why he wasn't hired.

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: So... what, did you hire a black man?

jokes.text: Well, you said you went a different way. I mean, how many different ways are there?
jokes.setup: Awkward silence after his racist comment
jokes.punchline: Hank doubles down with oblivious logic
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Hank's clueless persistence makes the cringe worse. His logic is absurdly reductive.

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: Well, you said you went a different way. I mean, how many different ways are there?

jokes.text: By things... by things, he means crying, by the way.
jokes.setup: Hank says he'd have to 'move some things around' to audition
jokes.punchline: Larry reveals Hank's 'things' are crying sessions
jokes.explanation: Excellent Larry putdown that reveals Hank's pathetic state. The casual aside delivery makes it more brutal.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: By things... by things, he means crying, by the way.

jokes.text: for those of you who are watching tonight, the unemployment line starts right to the right of pat.
jokes.setup: Pat Sajak offering Hank a possible job opportunity
jokes.punchline: Larry's cutting comment about where the unemployed should line up
jokes.explanation: Sharp meta-commentary on the end of the show. Larry's bitterness comes through in his mocking of job opportunities.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: for those of you who are watching tonight, the unemployment line starts right to the right of pat.

jokes.text: For those of you who are watching tonight, the unemployment line starts right to the right of pat.
jokes.setup: Larry is helping Hank network on air
jokes.punchline: Jokes about the unemployment line for desperate guests
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about the show becoming a job fair. Sharp and economical with good visual imagery.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: For those of you who are watching tonight, the unemployment line starts right to the right of pat.

jokes.text: the state of liberty. Uh, Hank? Merv? Pat. And, Hank, it's 'statue.' Statue of liberty.
jokes.setup: Hank hosting the game show
jokes.punchline: Multiple errors: wrong name, wrong monument name, and calling Pat 'Merv'
jokes.explanation: Perfect cascade of Hank errors. Each mistake compounds the incompetence, showing he's nervous and unprepared.

dialogue.speaker: Pat Sajak
dialogue.line: the state of liberty. Uh, Hank? Merv? Pat. And, Hank, it's 'statue.' Statue of liberty.

jokes.text: What? Jeff. My name's Jeff. You called me Jim. I called... I'm sorry. Who's this fucking guy?
jokes.setup: Hank keeps calling the contestant by the wrong name
jokes.punchline: When corrected, he gets hostile about being corrected
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Hank's inability to handle criticism. His defensive anger makes it worse.

dialogue.speaker: Jeff
dialogue.line: What? Jeff. My name's Jeff. You called me Jim.

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: I called... I'm sorry. Who's this fucking guy?

jokes.text: The correct title is the new colossus, so I guess you should worry a little less about your name and a little more about these, uh... these questions.
jokes.setup: Contestant gets trivia question wrong after correcting Hank's name mistake
jokes.punchline: Hank uses the wrong answer to justify his rudeness about the name
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Hank's petty revenge for being corrected. The logic is perfectly twisted.

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: The correct title is the new colossus, so I guess you should worry a little less about your name and a little more about these, uh... these questions.

jokes.text: Well, not to worry, debbie, with a body like yours, you don't have to be smart.
jokes.setup: Female contestant can't answer question
jokes.punchline: Hank's completely inappropriate sexual comment
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment showing Hank's complete inappropriateness. Predictable but effective character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: Well, not to worry, debbie, with a body like yours, you don't have to be smart.

jokes.text: Because, in fact, her tits aren't that great. I mean, they're too big for her body.
jokes.setup: Hank defending his compliment to the woman
jokes.punchline: He makes it worse by critiquing her body in detail
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Hank's inappropriate behavior - trying to defend himself makes it worse.

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: Because, in fact, her tits aren't that great. I mean, they're too big for her body.

jokes.text: I'm this close to hitting you.
jokes.setup: Larry has been giving Hank a pep talk
jokes.punchline: Reveals his true feelings about Hank's neediness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - the fake supportive facade cracking to reveal his true irritation. Maximum economy and impact.

dialogue.speaker: Larry Sanders
dialogue.line: I'm this close to hitting you.

jokes.text: That's down near San diego. No. Missouri.
jokes.setup: Andy mentions building a theater in Branson, Missouri
jokes.punchline: Hank confidently places Missouri near San Diego
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hank's ignorance masked as confidence. Simple geography mistake well-executed.

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: That's down near San diego.
No.
Missouri.

jokes.text: 2 million fucking dollars?
jokes.setup: Andy mentions making $2 million in November
jokes.punchline: Hank's shocked, profane reaction on live television
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Hank's unprofessional reaction to success he wishes he had. The profanity on air adds to it.

dialogue.speaker: Hank Kingsley
dialogue.line: 2 million fucking dollars?

jokes.text: What about, uh, big Jew jamboree?
jokes.setup: Hank is brainstorming names for his Branson venue
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriately offensive suggestion
jokes.explanation: Shocking and economical. Larry's casual anti-Semitism delivered as a helpful suggestion is perfectly inappropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: What about, uh, big Jew jamboree?

tmdb_overview: Larry's staff prepares for the end by secretly looking for new jobs.
